name: Larry Shields
abstract: Lawrence James Shields (September 13, 1893 – November 21, 1953) was an early American dixieland jazz clarinetist. He was a member of the Original Dixieland Jazz Band, the first jazz band to record commercially.

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: Shields collaborated from the beginning... But his name was on the Ostrich Walk... Shields collaborated. And on the numbers that Shields collaborated, he's got his name on there.

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Coquille
about_quotes.evidence: I really feel very good of having had the opportunity of working with fellas like the late Monk Hable, the late Larry Shields, and Jeff Riddick

about_quotes.speaker: Henry A. "Hank" Kmen
about_quotes.evidence: And at that time, I met Larry Shields. I met him through New Orleans musicians that I had met and worked with. I met him specifically.

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: Ray Lopez, Larry Shields, Illinois, Canada, and William Lambert were in the band with me.

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: Between Shields and myself... cooperative band... Shields put the lace on it

about_quotes.speaker: Armand Hug
about_quotes.evidence: The drummer was Von Gammon at the time I went there... And Harry Shields. Larry Shields' brother was playing clarinet... I wouldn't play the melodies. He said, Just play the chords... Harry Shields had a wonderful ear... Harry would say, No, no, that's the wrong change.
